The Giver (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $45,090,374Details
International Box Office $21,449,831
Worldwide Box Office $66,540,205
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $9,267,597
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,998,870
Total $11,266,467
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Jonas is a young man who lives in a seemingly ideal, if colorless, world of conformity and contentment. Yet as he begins to spend time with The Giver, who is the sole keeper of all the community’s memories, Jonas quickly begins to discover the dark and deadly truths of his community’s secret past. With this newfound power of knowledge, he realizes that the stakes are higher than imagined—a matter of life and death for himself and those he loves most. At extreme odds, Jonas knows that he must escape their world to protect them all—a challenge that no one has ever succeeded at before.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$12,305,016 (27.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.66 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:67.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,003 opening theaters/3,003 max. theaters, 5.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $66,007,455

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 15th, 2014 (Wide) by Weinstein Co.
International Releases: September 12th, 2014 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: November 25th, 2014 by Anchor Bay Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for a mature thematic image and some sci-fi action/violence.
(Rating bulletin 2332, 7/23/2014)
Running Time: 97 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Retro Black and White, Unnamed Character
Plot point: Coming of Age, Faulty Memory, First Love, Government Corruption, Romance

Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Tonik, As Is Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Jonas is a young man who lives in a seemingly ideal, if colorless, world of conformity and contentment. Yet as he begins to spend time with The Giver, who is the sole keeper of all the community’s memories, Jonas quickly begins to discover the dark and deadly truths of his community’s secret past. With this newfound power of knowledge, he realizes that the stakes are higher than imagined—a matter of life and death for himself and those he loves most. At extreme odds, Jonas knows that he must escape their world to protect them all—a challenge that no one has ever succeeded at before.
